pattern: Since every value can be nudged into any spot within its own window of size 2k, the largest group of values that can all be made equal is just the widest run of sorted values that fits inside one such window.
hints: Once each value is replaced with something in [value - k, value + k], two original values a and b can be made equal exactly when their difference is at most 2 * k.
Sort the array first -- then the values that can all become equal to each other form a contiguous stretch of the sorted array, so you only need to consider contiguous windows.
Slide a window over the sorted array, shrinking it from the left whenever the gap between its two ends exceeds 2 * k, and track the largest window width you ever reach.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, whole array already equal
k zero with all distinct values, only duplicates count
k zero but with existing duplicates already equal
large k lets the whole spread collapse together
k just one short of closing the widest gap
k exactly closes the widest gap

stress.description: random values and k across the full allowed ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    return [nums, k]
